source_jobs.title: Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT STRELLA

Strella is reinventing qualitative research with AI. Our platform conducts AI moderated interviews and synthesizes insights - automatically. Researchers, designers, marketers, and strategy teams at companies like Square and Duolingo use Strella to get faster, deeper insights from their customers. We’re backed by top-tier investors and on track to 4x revenue this year.


We’re looking for a Marketing Manager (2–3 years experience) to own how Strella shows up in the world through events, content, and thought leadership. This is a high-visibility role for someone who’s creative, scrappy, and eager to take full ownership in a fast-moving startup.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Plan and execute Strella’s presence at industry conferences, community events, and hosted gatherings

 - Own the content calendar (blogs, newsletters, LinkedIn, customer spotlights)

 - Repurpose events / webinars into scalable assets (recaps, clips, short videos)

 - Write posts / articles for Strella’s online presence

 - Collect customer stories and quotes for marketing campaigns and case studies

 - Partner with Growth to tie events and content into pipeline campaigns




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 2–3 years of B2B marketing experience (content and / or events)

 - Excellent writing and storytelling skills

 - Strong project manager who thrives in a fast-paced, startup environment

 - Creative and resourceful - you can make a little budget go a long way




WHY STRELLA?

 - Early, high-impact role with direct founder visibility

 - Shape Strella’s brand and voice from the ground up

 - Competitive salary, equity, and benefits

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT STRELLA

Strella is transforming how businesses gather and use customer insights with AI-moderated interviews and real-time synthesis, delivering human insights in hours instead of weeks. Our mission is to make fast, high-quality customer insights accessible to every business. We are a venture-backed startup and we’re looking to grow our team significantly in 2025 - join us!


We are seeking a Full Stack Software Engineer who embodies both technical excellence and a relentless drive to elevate our products. As one of our early engineers, you will play a large role in building our first customer-facing product.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Develop and maintain high-quality frontend and backend software solutions.

 - Take full ownership of projects, from conception to deployment, ensuring robustness, scalability, and minimal technical debt.

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams to translate needs into technical solutions.

 - Proactively improve existing systems, anticipating high-value projects and executing them autonomously.



YOU ARE A GOOD FIT FOR THIS ROLE IF

 - You have a strong bias for speed and action. You ship high-quality products daily, independently resolving bugs and roadblocks

 - You demonstrate exceptional proactivity in system and process improvements, autonomously executing high-value projects and delivering comprehensive solutions for large problems.

 - You are knowledgeable about tools and strategies for quality product development, capable of independent project architecture with occasional support.

 - You are a team player who isn’t afraid to work cross-functionally and exercise their product thinking skills

 - You solve problems with first principles thinking and always come with solutions



QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor's/Master’s degree in Computer Science or a related field.

 - 3+ years of experience in full-stack development. Particularly, significant experience leading projects.

 - Strong knowledge of CS fundamental concepts and web development

 - Familiar with languages like JavaScript, Typescript, Node.js, React, REST APIs, PostgreSQL databases, Tailwind CSS, Vercel, GCP

 - Highly comfortable with LLM prompt engineering in production software systems

 - Proficiency in designing scalable, robust, and efficient software architectures. This includes making decisions about the overall structure of the application, selecting appropriate design patterns, and foreseeing potential future needs or changes.

 - Strong problem-solving and communication skills.



BENEFITS

 - Competitive salary

 - Health Insurance (Medical, Dental, Vision)

 - Flexible PTO



COMP

 - Let’s talk

source_jobs.title: Product Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Strella

Strella is reinventing qualitative research with AI. Our platform conducts AI moderated interviews and synthesizes insights - automatically. Researchers, designers, marketers, and strategy teams at companies like Square and Duolingo use Strella to get faster, deeper insights from their customers. We’re backed by top-tier investors and have a track record of 25% month-over-month growth!



We’re looking for a Product Marketing Manager (4 – 5 years of experience) to own how Strella positions and presents itself to existing customers and the broader market. This is a high-visibility role for someone who’s creative, scrappy, and is excited to shape positioning and product storytelling in a fast-moving startup.

What You’ll Do

 - Own Strella’s changelog and education hub, ensuring every new feature is clearly explained and easy for customers to adopt

 - Lead lifecycle marketing for existing customers, including product release emails, newsletters, and product communications

 - Stay close to our product roadmap, acting as the glue between engineering, GTM, and the customer

 - Own and update website messaging, including positioning, value propositions, and product language as the product evolves

 - Document and maintain Strella’s messaging and positioning frameworks (e.g., ICPs, use cases, core narratives)

You’re a Great Fit for This Role if You…

 - Have an ownership mentality and are comfortable being the single-threaded owner of key workstreams

 - Can zoom out to the big picture (positioning, narrative, strategy) while also staying close to execution and details

 - Are a strong organizer and project manager who can juggle multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment

 - Have 4 – 5 years of B2B marketing experience, ideally in SaaS or a startup environment

 - Have excellent writing and storytelling skills

Why Strella?

 - Early, high-impact role with direct founder visibility

 - Shape Strella’s brand and voice from the ground up

 - Competitive salary, equity, and benefits

source_jobs.title: Strategy & Operations Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT STRELLA

Strella is an AI-native Voice of Customer (VoC) platform built for private equity teams and their portfolio companies. We combine primary research (including AI-moderated interviews) with automated analysis to turn customer conversations into clear, decision-ready insights—faster and with less manual effort than traditional surveys, expert calls, or spreadsheet-heavy synthesis.



We’re building the automation layer for market work in PE: a system that makes it easy to source feedback, run research, synthesize findings, and turn insights into action across diligence and value creation. The goal is always-on, defensible customer truth that plugs into the PE workflow and helps teams move from “collect data” to “make the call” with speed and confidence.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own 2–4 high-impact, ambiguous workstreams at a time (e.g., diligence research ops, customer onboarding, GTM ops, partnerships), from problem framing → execution → measurable outcomes.

 - Be customer-facing: join calls, run working sessions, and translate customer pain into clear product and go-to-market priorities.

 - Influence strategy and the roadmap: synthesize customer + market signals into crisp recommendations on ICP, pricing/packaging, and what we build next.

 - Build scalable systems: partner with engineering to automate workflows and ship tooling that removes repetitive work over time.

 - Turn repeatable wins into a playbook: codify best practices, templates, and “how we do this” docs that make the team faster.

 - Act as a force multiplier for the founders—identify the bottleneck, then fix it.




WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Experience: 3–6 years total (or equivalent), with demonstrated ownership of ambiguous, fast-moving workstreams.

 - Background: consulting, strategy & ops, product, growth, analytics, or customer-facing roles—especially in high-velocity environments (consulting, startups, or scrappy teams). Exposure to PE is a plus, not a requirement.

 - Execution strength: proven ability to drive projects from 0→1 with minimal oversight, strong judgment, and high accountability.

 - Analytical + structured: comfortable with Sheets/Excel, basic data analysis, and turning messy inputs into clear storylines and recommendations.

 - Communication: clear writer and facilitator; able to align stakeholders and move decisions forward without heavy process.

 - Builder mindset: bias toward action, high attention to detail, and a willingness to jump in wherever the work is highest-leverage.




NICE TO HAVE

 - Exposure to private equity, diligence, or voice-of-customer / research workflows.

 - Experience working closely with engineers (spec writing, QA, iterating on tooling).

 - Comfort prototyping with AI tools (e.g., “vibe coding” lightweight scripts/workflows).



WHY THIS ROLE

 - Work directly with the founders on company-defining initiatives.

 - Own meaningful projects end-to-end with high autonomy and high accountability.

 - Help build the operating system and playbooks that scale Strella.

 - High growth trajectory: opportunity to lead workstreams and manage a team over time.



COMPENSATION

Compensation is role-dependent and includes competitive cash, equity, and benefits. Let’s talk.

notes: AI-native Voice of Customer platform with a small team and high growth rate
